name: Tehran Ministry of Defense Strike
description: Combined US/Israeli strike on Iran's Ministry of Defense compound in central Tehran as part of opening salvo of Operation Epic Fury.

name: MOIS Headquarters Strike
description: Precision strike on Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security headquarters in Tehran. Secretary Ali Shamkhani among confirmed killed.

name: AEOI Headquarters Strike
target: Atomic Energy Organization of Iran HQ
description: Strike on AEOI headquarters in Tehran, targeting Iran's civilian nuclear administration.

name: Parchin Military Complex Strike
casualties: IRGC Ground Forces Commander Gen. Mohammad Pakpour killed
description: Massive strike on Parchin military-industrial complex southeast of Tehran. IRGC Ground Forces Commander-in-Chief among confirmed killed.

name: Supreme Leader Compound Strike
casualties: Ali Khamenei killed, Abdul Rahim Mousavi killed
description: Decapitation strike on the Supreme Leader's compound in Tehran. Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and Armed Forces Chief of Staff Abdul Rahim Mousavi confirmed killed.

name: IRGC Headquarters Strike
description: Strike on IRGC central headquarters in Tehran as part of the systematic targeting of Iran's military command structure.

name: Isfahan Military Complex Strike
description: Strikes on military-industrial targets around Isfahan, Iran's second major defense production hub.

name: Bushehr Area Strikes
description: Strikes on military installations in the Bushehr province, separate from the civilian nuclear power plant.

name: Konarak Naval Base Strike
casualties: Base largely destroyed, multiple IRIN vessels sunk at berth
description: Combined air and naval strike on Konarak Naval Base in southeastern Iran, a key IRIN facility on the Gulf of Oman.

name: Chabahar Port Military Strike
description: Strikes on military port infrastructure at Chabahar, Iran's strategic southeastern port near the Pakistan border.

name: Qom Military Targets Strike
description: Strikes on IRGC command and control facilities in the Qom area.

name: Tabriz Air Defense Strike
description: SEAD/DEAD strikes on integrated air defense systems around Tabriz in northwestern Iran.

name: Minab Girls' School Strike (Civilian)
description: US Tomahawk strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh school in Minab, a converted military facility adjacent to the IRGC Asif Brigade HQ. Pentagon investigation confirmed a US Tomahawk targeting error. Iranian state media reported 175 civilian casualties including primary school girls.

name: B-2 Strike on Underground Missile Site (Natanz area)
description: B-2 Spirit stealth bombers flying round-trip from CONUS (Whiteman AFB) delivered GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators on hardened underground missile production sites. Part of 4-bomber strike package.

name: B-2 Strike on Underground Facility (Fordow area)
description: GBU-57 MOP strikes on hardened mountain facilities in the Fordow area by B-2 bombers, targeting suspected missile storage and production infrastructure.

name: Israeli Strikes on Tehran (Roaring Lion)
description: Israeli Air Force contribution to the combined strike package under Operation Roaring Lion, Israel's parallel codename for the joint operation.

name: Israeli Strikes on Isfahan
description: Israeli strikes on defense-industrial targets in the Isfahan area as part of Operation Roaring Lion.

name: Defense Minister Aziz Nasirzadeh Killed
description: Iranian Defence Minister Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh confirmed killed in strikes on the Defense Ministry compound in Tehran.

name: IRIS Jamaran Corvette Sunk
description: Iranian Navy corvette IRIS Jamaran sunk in the Strait of Hormuz area during engagement with US Navy forces. The Jamaran was Iran's first domestically built warship.

name: Strait of Hormuz Closure
description: IRGC Navy declared closure of the Strait of Hormuz to all shipping following the start of Operation Epic Fury. Mine-laying operations and fast attack craft deployed across the shipping lanes. ~21% of global oil transit affected.

name: Hormuz Mine-Laying Operations
description: IRGC Navy deployed naval mines across the Strait of Hormuz shipping channels as part of the blockade operation.

name: Iranian Missile Strike on Beit Shemesh, Israel
description: Iranian ballistic missile salvo targeting populated area of Beit Shemesh, Israel. Partially intercepted by Iron Dome and Arrow systems.

name: Iranian Strike on US 5th Fleet HQ, Bahrain
description: Ballistic missile and drone attack on Naval Support Activity Bahrain, headquarters of US 5th Fleet and US Naval Forces Central Command.

name: Iranian Strike on Ali al-Salem Air Base, Kuwait
description: Iranian missile and drone strikes targeting Ali al-Salem Air Base in Kuwait, a major US Air Force forward operating location.

name: Iranian Missile Strike on Riyadh
description: Iranian ballistic missile strike on Riyadh, targeting Saudi military and government infrastructure. Saudi Patriot systems engaged incoming missiles.

name: Iranian Strike on Jebel Ali, Dubai
target: Jebel Ali Port / military facilities, Dubai
description: Iranian missile strikes targeting the Jebel Ali area in Dubai, which hosts both commercial port infrastructure and US logistics facilities.

name: Iranian Strike on Duqm, Oman
description: Iranian strikes on Duqm, Oman, where the US has access agreements for military logistics and staging.

name: Iranian Strike on Erbil, Iraq
target: US facilities near Erbil, Kurdistan Region, Iraq
description: Iranian missile strikes targeting US military facilities near Erbil International Airport in the Kurdistan Region of Iraq.

name: Iranian Strike on Al Udeid, Qatar
casualties: Unknown — CENTCOM forward HQ engaged defenses
description: Iranian strikes targeting Al Udeid Air Base in Qatar, the forward headquarters of US Central Command and Combined Air Operations Center.

name: USS Abraham Lincoln CSG Operations
description: USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) Carrier Strike Group operating in the North Arabian Sea, providing air support for Operation Epic Fury and fleet defense.

name: USS Gerald R. Ford CSG Operations
description: USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN-78) Carrier Strike Group positioned in the Gulf of Oman, the largest concentration of American carrier aviation in the region in a generation.

name: Operation Midnight Hammer — Natanz
description: June 2025 nuclear strikes on Iran's primary uranium enrichment facility at Natanz as part of Operation Midnight Hammer. This earlier operation set the context for the February 2026 escalation.

name: Operation Midnight Hammer — Fordow
description: June 2025 strikes on the Fordow Fuel Enrichment Plant, built deep inside a mountain near Qom. Part of Operation Midnight Hammer targeting Iran's nuclear infrastructure.

name: Operation Midnight Hammer — Isfahan Nuclear
description: June 2025 strikes on Isfahan's Nuclear Technology Center, which housed the uranium conversion facility. Part of Operation Midnight Hammer.

name: Shiraz Air Defense Suppression
description: SEAD operations against integrated air defense systems around Shiraz as part of the opening wave of Epic Fury.

name: Bandar Abbas Naval/Military Strike
description: Strikes on Iranian naval facilities at Bandar Abbas, Iran's primary naval port on the Strait of Hormuz, targeting both regular Navy and IRGC Navy assets.

name: Dezful Missile Base Strike
description: Strikes on IRGC Aerospace Force underground missile storage and launch facilities in the Dezful area of Khuzestan province.

name: Kermanshah Military Strikes
description: Strikes on IRGC military facilities in western Iran near Kermanshah, a key logistics hub for Iran's western defenses.

name: Iranian Strike Toward Al-Tanf, Syria/Jordan Border
description: Iranian missile and drone strikes targeting the US garrison at Al-Tanf base on the Syria-Jordan border.

name: Semnan Space/Missile Center Strike
target: Semnan Space Launch Center / missile test facility
description: Strikes on the Semnan Space Launch Center, which also serves as a ballistic missile test and development facility.

name: Khatam al-Anbiya Air Defense Strike
target: Khatam al-Anbiya Air Defense HQ, Tehran
description: Opening strike on Iran's centralized air defense command to degrade coordinated response capability.

name: Houthi Conditional Deterrence — Red Sea
target: Commercial shipping in Red Sea / Bab el-Mandeb
description: As of Mar 11, Houthis have maintained a conditional deterrence posture in solidarity with Iran but have not launched attacks. Leader Abdul-Malik al-Houthi stated forces had 'hands on the trigger' on Mar 5, with rhetoric and demonstrations but no confirmed kinetic operations in the Red Sea or Bab el-Mandeb strait.

name: Hezbollah Rocket Barrage — Northern Israel
description: Hezbollah launched rocket and drone barrages against northern Israel in solidarity with Iran following Operation Epic Fury. This was the first Hezbollah rocket fire toward Israel since the November 2024 ceasefire, breaking the truce in response to the killing of Khamenei.

name: Iranian Cyber Retaliation
description: Iranian state-sponsored cyber operations targeting US and Israeli critical infrastructure, financial systems, and government networks in retaliation for Operation Epic Fury.

name: B-2 Strike Package — Whiteman AFB Launch
target: Launch point for B-2 round-trip mission
description: Four B-2 Spirit stealth bombers launched from Whiteman Air Force Base, Missouri for a round-trip mission to Iran carrying GBU-57 Massive Ordnance Penetrators. Total mission time ~30 hours with aerial refueling.

name: IRGC Malek-Ashtar Building Destroyed
description: US/Israeli strike destroyed the IRGC Malek-Ashtar Building in Tehran. Video footage confirmed complete destruction of the structure.

name: IRIB State Broadcaster HQ Strike
description: Israel Air Force conducted a separate operation striking the IRIB (Islamic Republic of Iran Broadcasting) State Broadcaster headquarters in Tehran during early morning hours.

name: Wide-Scale Tehran + Isfahan Government Strikes
target: Iranian government infrastructure, Tehran and Isfahan
description: Wide-scale US/Israeli strikes targeting infrastructure of the Iranian government across Tehran and Isfahan. IDF confirmed targeting 'infrastructure of the Iranian government' as part of continuing combined operations.

name: IRGC Air Force HQ Destroyed
description: Israel Defense Forces confirmed the destruction of the IRGC Air Force headquarters in Tehran as part of ongoing Operation Roaring Lion strikes.

name: Overnight Strikes — Tehran and Northern Iran
description: Overnight US/Israeli strikes on Tehran and northern Iran as part of the ongoing campaign. By this point, over 5,000 total targets had been struck since the start of Operation Epic Fury.

name: Iranian Strikes on Dubai and Abu Dhabi
target: UAE military and infrastructure targets, Dubai and Abu Dhabi
description: IRGC Aerospace Force launched missile and drone strikes targeting military and infrastructure targets in Dubai and Abu Dhabi as part of the second wave of Iranian retaliation.

name: Iranian Strikes on Doha
description: Iranian missile strikes targeting Doha, Qatar as part of the second wave of IRGC retaliatory operations against Gulf states hosting US military assets.

name: Iranian Missile Strike on Beersheba
description: IRGC Aerospace Force targeted Beersheba in southern Israel with ballistic missiles as part of the second wave of Iranian retaliation.

name: US Embassy Kuwait Struck
description: Iranian strike hit the US Embassy compound in Kuwait City. The embassy was subsequently closed indefinitely.

name: Iran 500+ Missile / 2000 Drone Cumulative Barrage
description: Iran fired over 500 ballistic and naval missiles and 2,000 drones cumulatively. Fars News Agency confirmed approximately 40% aimed at Israel and 60% at US regional targets across the Persian Gulf.

name: Iranian Missiles at Tel Aviv
description: IRGC Aerospace Force launched ballistic missiles at Tel Aviv, triggering air raid alerts across central Israel. No immediate casualties reported as Israeli defense systems engaged incoming projectiles.

name: Iran's Most Intense Operation of the War
target: Israel, Persian Gulf nations, Hormuz shipping
description: Iran launched what was described as its most intense military operation of the war, simultaneously targeting Israel, Persian Gulf nations, and shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.

name: Hormuz Shipping Traffic Drops 70%
description: Shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz collapsed by 70% as commercial vessels diverted or anchored outside the strait. Over 150 ships were anchoring outside the strait, and by Mar 9 only 2 outbound Iranian-flagged vessels had transited.

name: Iran Allows China-Only Passage Through Hormuz
description: Iran established a selective passage regime through the Strait of Hormuz, allowing Chinese-flagged vessels to transit while blocking others. Iran had shipped 11.7 million barrels of oil to China since the start of the war.

name: Thai Cargo Ship Mayuree Naree Attacked
description: IRGC Navy attacked the Thai cargo ship Mayuree Naree in the Strait of Hormuz area. Two projectiles hit above the waterline, causing explosions in the stern and engine room. 20 crew members were rescued while 3 remained aboard.

name: Liberian-Flag Vessel Express Rome Attacked
description: IRGC Navy fired upon the Israeli-owned, Liberian-flagged vessel Express Rome for allegedly ignoring warnings. The IRGC named the vessel specifically in social media posts justifying the attack.

name: Third Vessel Struck in Persian Gulf
description: A third commercial vessel was struck in the Persian Gulf on March 11, making it one of the heaviest days of maritime attacks since the war began.

name: Kuwait F/A-18 Shoots Down 3 US F-15Es
target: Friendly fire — 3 US F-15E Strike Eagles
description: A Kuwaiti F/A-18 shot down 3 US F-15E Strike Eagles in a friendly fire incident during active Iranian missile and drone attacks. Highly active combat conditions contributed to misidentification. All 6 aircrew ejected safely and were recovered.

name: 3 US Service Members Killed, 5 Seriously Wounded
target: US forces — casualties from Iranian attacks
description: First US combat deaths in Operation Epic Fury. Three service members were killed and five seriously wounded in Iranian retaliatory attacks on US positions.

name: US Soldier Killed in Saudi Arabia
target: US forces — casualty from Iranian attack
description: A Kentucky-based US soldier died of injuries sustained in an Iranian attack on Saudi Arabia, adding to the first day's combat deaths.

name: US Casualties: 7 KIA, ~140 Wounded
casualties: 7 KIA, ~140 wounded (8 severe, 108 returned to duty), plus 1 health-related death in Kuwait
description: Pentagon confirmed cumulative US casualties as of Mar 10: 7 killed in action plus 1 health-related death in Kuwait, approximately 140 wounded with 8 severe injuries, and 108 who had returned to duty.

name: Iraqi Militia Drone Strikes on Erbil
target: Erbil International Airport area / US assets
description: Iraqi militia groups launched drone strikes on the Erbil International Airport area, claiming 23+ drone strikes on US assets in coordination with the broader Axis of Resistance response to Operation Epic Fury.

name: Victory Base Baghdad Attacked
target: US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport
description: Guardians of the Blood Brigade (Saraya Awliya Al-Dam) launched drone and rocket attacks on US Victory Base near Baghdad International Airport.

name: Kataib Hezbollah and Allies Formally Join Fighting
target: US and coalition forces in Iraq
description: Kataib Hezbollah and allied Iraqi militia groups formally announced their entry into the war, citing 'attacks on Iran and violations of Iraq's sovereignty' as justification.

name: Islamic Resistance in Iraq: 291 Total Attacks
target: US and coalition forces across Iraq
description: The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed a cumulative total of 291 attacks since the start of the war, including 31 new operations on Mar 10-11.

name: Hezbollah Formally Breaks November 2024 Ceasefire
description: Hezbollah launched its first rockets from Lebanon toward Israel since the November 27, 2024 ceasefire, formally breaking the truce in response to the killing of Khamenei in Operation Epic Fury.

name: Israel Strikes 500+ Targets Across Lebanon
description: Israel struck over 500 targets across Lebanon between Mar 2-6, targeting Hezbollah positions in response to the ceasefire breach. At least 50 people were killed and over 330 injured.

name: Israel Orders Evacuation South of Litani River
description: Israel ordered evacuation of all areas south of the Litani River, affecting 250-300 settlements including the cities of Tyre and Nabatieh. Widely seen as a precursor to ground invasion.

name: 500,000+ Displaced in Lebanon
description: Lebanese social affairs minister reported 517,000 registered displaced persons, 538 active shelters, 119,700 IDPs in shelters, and 667,831 self-registered displaced people across the country.

name: Israeli Ground Incursion Into Southern Lebanon
description: IDF ground forces entered the village of Qaouzah, approximately 2.75km north of the Blue Line, marking the furthest verified Israeli ground incursion into Lebanon. Intense clashes with Hezbollah reported.

name: Hezbollah Projectile Wounds 8 IDF Soldiers
casualties: 8 soldiers injured (5 severe), including Finance Minister Smotrich's son
description: A Hezbollah projectile wounded 8 IDF soldiers near the Lebanese border, including 5 with severe injuries. Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich's son was among the wounded.

name: Interim Leadership Council Formed
description: An Interim Leadership Council was established per the Iranian constitution to exercise head of state functions pending the election of a new supreme leader following the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei.

name: Mojtaba Khamenei Elected Supreme Leader
description: Mojtaba Khamenei, son of the killed Ali Khamenei, was elected as Iran's new Supreme Leader. The IRGC reportedly pressured the Assembly of Experts. Russia pledged 'unwavering' support while Trump called it 'unacceptable' and Israel vowed to target him.

name: UN Security Council Emergency Session
description: China and Russia requested an emergency UN Security Council session, citing an 'unprovoked and reckless act of military aggression' and calling for an immediate ceasefire. The US and Israel were diplomatically isolated.

name: Brent Crude Hits $126/Barrel
description: Brent crude oil surged to $126 per barrel, the first time above $100 since 2022. The Hormuz closure was driving one of the biggest oil supply disruptions in history, forcing Gulf states to cut output.

name: IEA Agrees to Release Record 400M Barrels
description: The International Energy Agency agreed to release a record 400 million barrels from strategic petroleum reserves, the largest reserve release in IEA history — dwarfing the 182 million barrel response during the Ukraine crisis. Contributors included the UK (13.5M barrels), South Korea (22.46M), Germany, Austria, and Japan.

name: KC-135 Tanker Crash in Western Iraq — 6 Airmen Killed
description: A US Air Force KC-135 Stratotanker crashed in western Iraq while supporting Operation Epic Fury. All six crew members were killed. CENTCOM confirmed the loss was not due to hostile or friendly fire. First loss of a KC-135 in 13 years.

name: Iranian Drones Hit Bahrain Fuel Tanks at Muharraq
target: Bahrain International Airport — fuel tanks, Muharraq
casualties: Unknown — fire set in fuel tanks
description: Iranian drones struck fuel tanks at Muharraq near Bahrain International Airport, setting them ablaze. Bahrain Defense Force reported cumulative interception of 114 missiles and 190 drones since the war began. Residents told to stay inside and close windows.

name: US Strikes PMF at Qaim — 99 Killed
target: PMF 19th Brigade (Ansar Allah al Awfiya) — HQ, medical unit, logistics
description: US airstrikes targeted three PMF 19th Brigade sites in the Qaim border district of Anbar province. PMF reported 32 total US airstrikes on militia HQs across 7 Iraqi governorates since Feb 28.

name: Saudi Arabia Intercepts 31 Drones and 3 Ballistic Missiles
target: Saudi Arabia — Eastern Province, Shaybah oilfield
description: Saudi air defenses intercepted and destroyed 31 drones and three ballistic missiles targeting the kingdom, including waves aimed at the Eastern Province and Shaybah oilfield.

name: Saudi Arabia Targeted With 50 Drones — 2 Killed in al-Kharj
target: Saudi Arabia — Eastern Province, Riyadh, al-Kharj
casualties: 2 killed, 12 injured in al-Kharj residential area
description: Iran launched 50 drones at Saudi Arabia within hours. Saudi intercepted 51 drones including over Riyadh. A projectile fell in a residential area in al-Kharj governorate, killing 2 and injuring 12.

name: US Strikes Kataib Hezbollah and Asaib Ahl al Haq
target: Asaib Ahl al Haq HQ (Salah al Din), Kataib Hezbollah HQ (Fallujah)
description: US airstrikes targeted an Asaib Ahl al Haq headquarters in Salah al Din province and a Kataib Hezbollah headquarters in Fallujah, continuing the campaign against Iran-backed militias in Iraq.

name: Heaviest Single-Night Bombardment of Iran
target: Isfahan, Shiraz, Dezful, Tehran, Tabriz, Hamedan, Qom, Sirjan, Garmsar
casualties: At least 15 killed in Isfahan; 66+ total casualties
description: Heaviest single-night bombardment since Epic Fury began. Strikes hit military and state-linked sites across 9+ Iranian cities. Isfahan IRGC airbase, Dezful 4th Tactical Air Base hit. Over 20 explosions heard near Shiraz alone.

name: Iran FM Rejects Ceasefire: 'We Never Asked'
description: Iranian FM Abbas Araghchi rejected ceasefire talks: 'We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked for negotiation. We are ready to defend ourselves as long as it takes.' Trump had claimed Iran was willing to negotiate.

name: Israel Launches Full Ground Invasion of Southern Lebanon
target: Southern Lebanon — south of Litani River
casualties: 1,000+ killed in Lebanon since conflict began
description: IDF launched 'limited and targeted ground operations' in southern Lebanon, aiming to seize the entire area south of the Litani River. Heavy fighting around strategic Khiam stronghold. IDF destroyed a bridge over the Litani River.

name: Iranian Drone Hits Dubai Airport Fuel Tank — 7hr Shutdown
description: An Iranian drone struck a fuel tank at Dubai International Airport, one of the world's busiest hubs, sparking a fire. The airport suspended operations for 7+ hours. UAE intercepted 13 ballistic missiles and 27 drones from Iran during the same period.

name: Hormuz Commercial Transit Collapses to Zero — 300+ Ships Stranded
target: Strait of Hormuz — all commercial shipping
description: Commercial transit through the Strait of Hormuz collapsed to effectively zero, with over 300 ships stranded inside the Persian Gulf. Only a small number of negotiated safe voyages occurred. Crisis affected ~20% of global daily oil supply.

name: 1 Million Displaced in Lebanon — 1/5 of Population
casualties: 1M displaced (19% of population), 119K Syrians crossed to Syria
description: Lebanon displacement reached 1 million — one-fifth of the country's population. By March 18, 119,000 Syrians crossed back into Syria with 125,000 total crossings. Over 1,000 fatalities in Lebanon since the conflict began.

name: US Casualties: 13 KIA, 200+ Wounded Across 7 Countries
casualties: 13 KIA (7 enemy fire + 6 KC-135 crash), 200+ wounded
description: US casualties reached 13 dead and over 200 wounded across 7 countries as confirmed by CENTCOM. Deaths include 7 killed by enemy fire and 6 airmen in the KC-135 crash. Pentagon warned of likely additional losses.

name: Israel Kills Ali Larijani and Basij Commander Soleimani
target: Ali Larijani (SNSC Secretary), Gholamreza Soleimani (Basij Commander)
casualties: Larijani + son Morteza + office head + guards; Soleimani killed
description: Israeli airstrikes killed Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council — the most senior official killed after Khamenei — and Basij paramilitary commander Gholamreza Soleimani. Larijani was a key JCPOA negotiator and former parliament speaker.

name: Israel Strikes South Pars Gas Field at Asaluyeh
target: South Pars gas field — Asaluyeh processing hub
description: Israeli drone strike hit Asaluyeh, the onshore processing hub for Iran's South Pars gas field — the world's largest natural gas reserve shared with Qatar. Four treatment plants damaged, ~100M cubic meters/day offline (14% of output). South Pars supplies 70% of Iran's domestic gas. Trump criticized Israel for the strike.

name: Iran Strikes Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG — World's Largest
target: Ras Laffan Industrial City — world's largest LNG facility
description: Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan gas-to-liquid facility in retaliation for the South Pars attack, causing 'extensive damage.' Ras Laffan produces ~20% of global LNG supply. Qatar forced to halt all gas production, disrupting global supplies and fertilizer production.

name: Iran Strikes Energy Facilities Across UAE and Saudi Arabia
target: UAE Al Hosn gasfield, Saudi Samref/Jubail refineries, Qatar Mesaieed
description: Iran launched strikes at five named energy targets across the Gulf: Qatar's Mesaieed Petrochemical, Saudi Samref Refinery and Jubail Petrochemical, UAE's Al Hosn gasfield. Saudi intercepted 4 ballistic missiles toward Riyadh. UAE intercepted 13 missiles and 27 drones.

name: Iran Launches 19 Attack Waves at Israel — Record Day
description: Iran launched 19 ballistic missile attack waves at Israel in a single day — an exceptional number far exceeding the daily average. This was one of the most intense days of Iranian attacks on Israel during the entire conflict.

name: Brent Crude Touches $119 — Analysts Warn of $200
description: Brent crude briefly touched $119/barrel after Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan. Analysts raised the prospect of $200/barrel. Oil surged ~80% since the conflict began. Gulf states cut total oil production by at least 10M barrels/day due to the Hormuz closure.

name: Qatar Forced to Halt All Gas Production
target: Qatar LNG production — 20% of global supply
description: Qatar, one of the world's biggest LNG producers, halted all gas production after Iranian attacks on Ras Laffan. This disrupted 20% of global LNG supply. Qatar PM: 'This war must be stopped immediately.'

name: UK + 5 Allies Demand Iran Stop Attacks, End Hormuz Blockade
description: UK PM Starmer joined Japan, Germany, France, Italy, and the Netherlands in calling on Iran to stop attacks on energy sites and civilian infrastructure and end the Hormuz gridlock. Five Western nations condemned Hezbollah's decision to join hostilities.

name: US Strikes PMF Across Baghdad, Nineveh, Anbar
target: PMF militia bases — Baghdad, Salah al Din, Nineveh, Anbar
description: US airstrikes targeted Iran-backed militia bases across four Iraqi governorates. PMF condemned targeting of HQs in Anbar and near Mosul. Iraqi PM condemned the attacks. A base at Tuz Khurmatu in central Iraq was also struck.

name: IDF Strikes 130+ Targets Across Iran on Nowruz
target: Tehran, Parchin, Kerman, Arak, Bandar Lengeh, Yazd, Ahvaz, Karaj
description: Israel struck 130+ regime infrastructures on Nowruz (Persian New Year) — missile launchers, UAV sites, air defense systems across 8+ cities. CENTCOM struck a missile production facility in Karaj. US has hit 7,000+ total targets since Feb 28.

name: Iranian Drones Hit Kuwait Mina Al-Ahmadi Refinery
description: Iranian drones struck Kuwait's Mina Al-Ahmadi oil refinery overnight, sparking fires. Saudi Arabia intercepted a missile over the northern Al Jouf region during the same period.

name: Iran Death Toll Exceeds 1,444 — 204 Children Killed
description: Iranian Red Crescent reported 204 children killed as overall death toll exceeded 1,444. Independent Hengaw documented 5,300+ dead including 511 civilians in first 18 days. Over 42,000 civilian sites damaged including homes, schools, hospitals.

name: Trump Calls NATO Allies 'Cowards' Over Hormuz
target: NATO alliance — pressure for Hormuz coalition
description: President Trump criticized NATO allies for lacking 'enthusiasm' to help with the Hormuz crisis, calling them 'cowards.' He said 'many countries' would send warships. Netanyahu claimed Israel was helping to open the Strait of Hormuz. Pentagon requested $200B additional war funding.
